name: Frank Capp
abstract: Francis Cappuccio (August 20, 1931 – September 12, 2017), known professionally as Frank Capp, was an American jazz drummer. Capp also played on numerous rock and roll sessions and is considered to be a member of the Wrecking Crew.

interviews.title: Frank Capp interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald, Singer

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: But there’s guys that have been working for me for thirty years now, when I put the band together. And so every once in a while, that cliché, when they make me an offer I can’t refuse. But now they’re all leaders by the way. Med Flory, Frank Capp.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Capp is mentioned only as a member of Pat Boone's band ('...has Frank Capp, Barney Kessel and Ray Brown and me for a rhythm section'), with no direct interaction or relationship described between the interviewee (Paul Smith) and Frank Capp.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: So I remember we were in Concord and Don Menza and Richie Kamuca and Jack Nimitz and Marshall Royal was playing lead alto. And Lanny Morgan was playing. And Dave Frishberg and Monty Budwig and Frank Capp. So we got a hot band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Capp was the drummer and whatever. You know. So all these guys... And they’re all saying Jeez, who is this guy you know?
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Bergeron
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Capp was really fun to play with because he had a hard swinging right hand
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Bergeron interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2020
